welcome.title: Welcome to Wavedge!
welcome.description: Your crypto intelligence dashboard is ready. Here's how to get started:
heatmap.title: Signal Heatmap
heatmap.tip: Signals = a composite indicator of news volume + social discussion + whale activity. More signals means this token is worth watching.
heatmap.legendSizeTip: Larger cells = higher market cap. The biggest tokens take up the most space.
heatmap.legendNewsTip: Number of news articles in the last 24 hours. Arrows show whether sentiment is bullish or bearish.
heatmap.legendSocialTip: Social media mentions and sentiment from Twitter, Reddit, and Telegram.
heatmap.legendWhaleTip: Large transactions (whale activity) detected on-chain in the last 24 hours.
feed.historicalTooltip: Historical avg 24h price change based on {samples} similar event{plural}
stats.priceTip: The percentage price change in the last 24 hours.
stats.sentimentTip: Market Sentiment: Bullish = people are optimistic, Bearish = people are pessimistic.
stats.marketCapTip: The total value of all circulating coins. Higher means the market is bigger.
chart.selectToken: Select a token to view chart
chart.chartTip: Candlestick chart: each candle represents the open, high, low, close price for a time period. Green = up, Red = down.
chart.clickToLoad: Click a token row to load its price chart
table.marketCapTip: The total value of all circulating coins. Higher means the market is bigger.
table.volumeTip: 24h Volume: the total amount of trading in the past day. Higher means a more active market.
table.noData: No price data yet — updates every 5 minutes
impact.impactTip: Based on historical data, the average price change within 24 hours after similar news was published. (n=sample size)
impact.emptyAction: Add tokens to your watchlist to see related news
impact.emptyAddTokens: Add more tokens to your watchlist
alerts.watchlistDesc: Select tokens you want to receive alerts for.
alerts.channelsDesc: Choose how you want to receive alerts.
alerts.channelPushDesc: Browser push alerts even when tab is closed
alerts.telegramHelp: Send /start to @WavedgeBot to get your chat ID
alerts.sensitivityTip: Higher sensitivity triggers alerts more easily. Medium is recommended for beginners.
alerts.sensitivityDesc: Controls how easily alerts are triggered. Higher sensitivity = more alerts.
alerts.signalRequirementsTip: How many signals must fire simultaneously to trigger an alert. Beginners should choose 2-3 to reduce noise.
alerts.signalRequirementsDesc: How many signals must fire simultaneously to trigger an alert.
alerts.pushNotSupported: Push notifications are not supported in this browser
alerts.pushNotConfigured: Push notifications not configured on server
alertHistory.noAlerts: No alerts triggered in the last {hours} hours.
alertHistory.explanation: Alerts fire when multiple signals (news, price, volume) breach thresholds simultaneously.
upgrade.title: Upgrade to Pro
upgrade.description: Unlock {feature} and more with Wavedge Pro.
missed.proDesc: Pro users got them all. Free plan: {limit} alerts/day.
stale.delayed: Data may be delayed (last update: {age} ago)
watchlist.signalsTip: The number of news articles and events related to this token in the past 24 hours.
watchlist.emptyTitle: No tokens on your watchlist yet
ad.upgradePro: Upgrade to Pro — no ads, real-time alerts, full history
apiKey.loginRequired: Please log in to manage API keys.
apiKey.proDesc: Generate API keys to access Wavedge data programmatically. Upgrade to Pro to unlock API access.
apiKey.loadFailed: Failed to load API keys. Please try again.
apiKey.copyWarning: Copy this key now — it won't be shown again.
apiKey.noKeys: No active API keys. Create one above.
apiKey.quickStartDesc: Use your API key as a Bearer token. Rate limit: 100 requests/day.
apiKey.revokeConfirm: Revoke this API key? Any applications using it will stop working.
signal.hasArticles: {symbol} has {count} news articles in the last 24 hours
signal.noActiveSignals: No active signals for {symbol} right now
signal.newsTip: News articles mentioning this token in the last 24 hours. Normal: <3 articles/hr, High: 3-5, Very High: >5.
signal.socialTip: Aggregated sentiment from social media (Twitter, Reddit, Telegram). Score ranges from -1 (very bearish) to +1 (very bullish).
signal.whaleTip: Large on-chain transfers (typically >$100K) detected in the last 24 hours. High whale activity can signal upcoming price movement.
signal.impactTip: Shows how similar news events have historically affected this token's price within 24 hours.
signal.impactExplanation: Based on {samples} historical events, {category} news has moved {symbol} an average of {change} within 24 hours.
impact.impactExplanation: Based on {samples} historical events, {category} news has moved {symbol} an average of {change} within 24 hours.
explainer.heatmap.title: Signal Heatmap
explainer.heatmap.body: This color grid shows the top 50 tokens. Green means the price went up, red means it went down. Bigger tiles are bigger tokens (by market cap). Badges show news activity, social buzz, and large transactions.
explainer.impact.title: Impact Feed
explainer.impact.body: Each news article is scored by how much similar news has historically moved the token's price. A higher impact score means this type of news has caused bigger price swings in the past.
explainer.watchlist.title: Watchlist & Signals
explainer.watchlist.body: Your watchlist tracks tokens you care about. The "Signals" column counts how many news articles and events happened in the last 24 hours — more signals means more is happening with that token.
glossary.title: Crypto Glossary
glossary.token.def: A digital asset on a blockchain. Bitcoin (BTC) and Ethereum (ETH) are the two largest tokens by value.
glossary.marketCap.def: The total value of all coins in circulation. Calculated as: price × circulating supply. A higher market cap usually means a more established token.
glossary.volume.def: The total amount of trading activity in the last 24 hours. High volume means many people are buying and selling.
glossary.priceChange.def: How much the price has gone up or down in the last 24 hours, shown as a percentage. +5% means the price rose 5%.
glossary.bullish.def: When people expect prices to go up. A bullish market means optimism and rising prices.
glossary.bearish.def: When people expect prices to go down. A bearish market means pessimism and falling prices.
glossary.whale.def: Someone who holds a very large amount of a token. When whales buy or sell, it can move the price significantly.
glossary.signal.def: An indicator that something notable is happening with a token — like a spike in news articles, social media buzz, or whale transactions.
glossary.impactScore.def: A number showing how much similar news has historically affected a token's price within 24 hours. Higher scores mean bigger historical price moves.
glossary.sentiment.def: The overall mood of the market or community about a token. Measured from social media and news as bullish (positive), bearish (negative), or neutral.
glossary.heatmap.def: A visual grid where color intensity shows price movement. Green = up, red = down. Tile size represents market cap.
glossary.watchlist.def: Your personal list of tokens to track. Wavedge monitors these tokens and sends you alerts when something important happens.
primer.title: New to crypto?
primer.tokensBody: Tokens (or coins) are digital money that runs on a blockchain. Bitcoin and Ethereum are the most well-known. Each token has a price that changes based on supply and demand.
primer.marketCapBody: Market cap = price × total coins in circulation. It tells you how big a token is overall. Bitcoin has the largest market cap, making it the "biggest" cryptocurrency.
primer.priceChangesBody: Crypto prices move when people buy (price goes up) or sell (price goes down). News, regulations, and big transactions ("whale moves") can all cause sudden price changes.
